
Interleaving 2 Montgomery Multiplications in one function to make the CPU
pipeline more busy while waiting for loop-carried dependency chain of one
of the modular multiplications.

LINE-PER-LINE-INTERLEAVED:

Latency verdict: 5196 microseconds.

================================================================================
13 May 2026 |
------------

Inner loop in Mont_MUL factored out into a static always_inline function, with
__restrict__ u64* pointers for ->bits, no T pointer (#define for T in R->bits)
and no setting pointers to bit buffers inbetween mulx and addcarryx intrinsics.

The interleaved version reuses the same modulus N (because it's always the same)
and reuses the locals Ul, Uh, Vl, Vh, carryC, carryD, W.


320-bit powers, so we have 320 + 160 Mont_MUL calls in 1 Mont_POW. Two POWs, so
960 Mont_MUL calls in total for 1 signature validation. L = 64-bit limbs in
3072-bit modulus, in Mont_MUL, outer loop is 0 to L, inner loop is 1 to L, so:
(48 * 47) * 960 ~ 2,165,000 inner loop iterations per signature validate.
